chapter: Chapter 10: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

questions.question: Why does the eye lens need to change its focal length?
questions.answer: To focus objects at different distances clearly on the retina.

questions.question: Why is the retina considered the most important part of the eye?
questions.answer: Because it contains light-sensitive cells that form images and convert them into nerve signals.

questions.question: Why can a normal human eye see distant objects clearly but not objects closer than 25 cm?
questions.answer: Because the eye lens cannot increase its curvature beyond a limit to focus very near objects.

questions.question: Why does myopia occur in the human eye?
questions.answer: Because the eyeball becomes elongated or the eye lens becomes too powerful.

questions.question: Why is a concave lens used to correct myopia?
questions.answer: Because it diverges light rays so the image forms on the retina.

questions.question: Why does hypermetropia occur?
questions.answer: Because the eyeball is too short or the eye lens has insufficient converging power.

questions.question: Why is a convex lens used to correct hypermetropia?
questions.answer: Because it converges light rays before they enter the eye to form the image on the retina.

questions.question: Why does presbyopia occur in old age?
questions.answer: Due to weakening of ciliary muscles and loss of elasticity of the eye lens.

questions.question: Why are bifocal lenses useful for people with presbyopia?
questions.answer: They allow clear vision for both near and distant objects.

questions.question: Why does white light split into many colours in a prism?
questions.answer: Because different colours of light have different wavelengths and refract differently.

questions.question: Why does violet light bend more than red light in a prism?
questions.answer: Because violet light has a shorter wavelength and higher refractive index.

questions.question: Why is the band of colours produced by dispersion called a spectrum?
questions.answer: Because it contains all the component colours of white light arranged in order.

questions.question: Why does scattering of light occur in the atmosphere?
questions.answer: Because light interacts with small particles and molecules present in the air.

questions.question: Why is blue light scattered more than red light?
questions.answer: Because shorter wavelengths scatter more strongly than longer wavelengths.

questions.question: Why does the sky appear blue during the daytime?
questions.answer: Because shorter wavelength blue light is scattered more by the atmosphere.

questions.question: Why does the sky appear dark to astronauts in space?
questions.answer: Because there is no atmosphere to scatter sunlight.

questions.question: Why does the Sun appear red during sunrise and sunset?
questions.answer: Because most shorter wavelengths are scattered and only red light reaches the observer.

questions.question: Why are danger signals red in colour?
questions.answer: Because red light has the longest wavelength and scatters the least.

questions.question: Why do stars appear to twinkle?
questions.answer: Due to atmospheric refraction of starlight by layers of air with varying density.

questions.question: Why do planets not twinkle like stars?
questions.answer: Because they appear as extended sources and atmospheric refraction averages out the fluctuations.

questions.question: Why does the Sun appear slightly flattened at sunrise and sunset?
questions.answer: Because of atmospheric refraction of sunlight.

questions.question: Why is the image formed on the retina inverted?
questions.answer: Because the eye lens acts as a convex lens forming a real inverted image.

questions.question: Why is the ability of accommodation limited in humans?
questions.answer: Because the elasticity of the eye lens has a natural limit.

questions.question: Why does fog appear white?
questions.answer: Because water droplets scatter all colours of light almost equally.

questions.question: Why does the Tyndall effect help us see the path of a light beam?
questions.answer: Because light is scattered by particles in the medium making the beam visible.
